title: Startup Self-Check
description: A reactive troubleshooting tutorial that helps the user get to a safe startup state before they need outside help.

steps.title: Startup Self-Check
steps.body: This troubleshooting tutorial inspects the current state of the app and routes you to the next useful step. It is designed to help you self-correct common startup issues.

steps.title: Engine Already Running
steps.body: The system already appears to be running. If you want to troubleshoot startup from a clean state, terminate the current session first. Otherwise, you can leave it running and finish this tutorial.

steps.title: LM Studio Needs Attention
steps.body: A usable model does not appear to be loaded yet. Start LM Studio, load a model, and keep this window open. This step will continue automatically when a model becomes available here.

steps.title: Apply a Safe Startup Baseline
steps.body: This step applies a conservative startup baseline: MuseTalk, Stream Mode On, Chatterbox, and Very Low VRAM. That does not lock you in forever. It just gives troubleshooting a reliable starting point.

steps.title: Load Tutorial Persona
steps.body: For this self-check flow, please load the bundled Tutorial Persona preset. That gives the assistant the right help context if you need to ask questions during testing.

steps.title: Initialize the System
steps.body: Click INITIALIZE SYSTEM. This step listens for a real startup signal from the app. If the engine reaches ready state, the tutorial will move forward automatically. If a startup error is detected, it will branch to a troubleshooting note.

steps.title: Startup Reported an Error
steps.body: The app reported an error during startup. Look at the Console tab for the latest message. Common causes are LM Studio not serving a model, an unavailable backend, or a GPU/VRAM mismatch. If you press Next, the tutorial will route you back into a safer recovery path instead of pretending startup succeeded.

steps.title: Startup Succeeded
steps.body: The app reported a real ready signal, so the system appears to have initialized successfully. From here you can start a conversation, explore the Tutorials tab, or later run Dry Run to optimize performance for this machine.
